name: AI & Prompt Services
description: Constructs and optimizes prompts sent to LLM models, abstracts Claude and Gemini API access, manages reusable skill blocks, and reduces context to stay within token limits.

name: App Infrastructure
description: Application entry point, composition root wiring all managers together, global configuration constants, structured logging to rotating log files, crash/hang detection, and the system tray icon lifecycle.

name: Codebase Intelligence
description: Understands the codebase by indexing symbols, discovering and registering logical features, maintaining module groupings, and providing hybrid keyword+vector search to surface relevant context for task prompts.

name: Data & History
description: Persists and retrieves all application data including project metadata, task history, active queue snapshots, project-level to-do lists, and reusable task templates and saved prompts.

name: External Integrations
description: Connects Spritely to external systems: the MCP-for-Unity server for in-editor automation, the file-based agent message bus for inter-task communication, and agent bus operation logging.

name: Feature Registry System
description: Maintains the per-project feature and module registry stored under `.spritely/features/`, including bootstrapping from codebase scans, resolving relevant features for a task, updating the registry post-completion, and a symbol-level codebase index.

name: Git Integration
description: Provides a safe wrapper around Git CLI operations (commit, status, diff) enforced by GitOperationGuard, and renders the git status panel with staged-file selection and commit workflow UI.

name: History & Persistence
description: Persists completed tasks, active queue snapshots, task groups, and application settings to disk, and exposes aggregated activity statistics and analytics across sessions.

name: Infrastructure & Logging
description: Bootstraps the WPF application, wires the composition root, handles unhandled exceptions, crash and hang detection, structured log rotation, safe background file writes, and the system tray icon.

name: Project & Configuration
description: Manages per-project metadata, rules, descriptions, and color assignments, alongside app-wide settings, constants, skills, and reusable task templates that configure agent behavior.

name: Prompt Building & AI Services
description: Assembles prompts from modular blocks, optimizes context to stay within token limits, drives multi-iteration feature-mode execution, coordinates inter-agent messaging, and provides streaming interfaces to Claude and Gemini APIs including MCP server integration.

name: Core Task Engine
description: Manages the full lifecycle of AI tasks from creation through execution, orchestration, and completion. Includes DAG-based dependency management, preprocessing, decomposition into subtasks, progress tracking, and file-lock coordination.

name: Task Execution & Orchestration
description: Manages the full lifecycle of AI agent tasks from creation, preprocessing, queuing, and dependency resolution through CLI invocation, monitoring, pause/unpause, and completion. Includes multi-iteration feature mode execution and output processing.

name: UI & Visualization
description: All user-facing WPF surfaces: main window composition, task cards and output tabs, interactive dependency graph, chat panel, terminal, activity dashboard, dialogs for stored tasks and helper suggestions, theming, and task data export.
